creator: bapt@FreeBSD.org
text:
What should be done is the maintainer should provide by default a sane screenrc via the @sample so that the user can modify this screenrc and his modification does not get overwritten by an upgrade.

As I'm not a user of screen I cannot argue whether the default provided by upstream screenrc is sane or not.

All I can say is most of the linux downstreams are often providing themselves a custom version of the default screenrc which tends to look pretty much like the one that has been added by default on FreeBSD (quite different from the upstream example)
